categories.title: Emotional Fine-Grained Control
categories.description: Given the same neutral text and an emotional speech prompt, the Refiner produces speech that simultaneously realizes the target emotion and applies fine-grained prosodic edits (word-level stress or pause insertion).
categories.text: Listen closely, the same words can tell a very different story.

categories.title: Refinement with Instruction
categories.description: The AudioLLM Judge diagnoses prosodic defects in the initial utterance and generates a structured refine instruction. The Refiner then corrects the speech accordingly, preserving speaker identity.

samples.text: I've told you 100 times—don't open the door at night.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Fearful emotion. Specifically, stress the word '100', insert a pause after 'times', stress the word 'don't', and stress the word 'night'.

samples.text: The letter slipped from his grasp, a paper cut of bitter news.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Sad emotion at a slow speed and low pitch. Specifically, stress the word 'slipped', insert a pause after 'grasp', and stress the word 'bitter'.

samples.text: The car door slammed shut, punctuating the end of the argument.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Angry emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'slammed', stress the word 'shut', and stress the word 'end', insert a pause after 'end'.

samples.text: I don't just understand you, Anne,' he said hesitatingly. 'You appear to be vexed about something.
samples.instruction: Specifically, stress the word 'understand', stress the word 'vexed', and insert a pause after 'Anne'.

samples.text: Upon trying her hand at painting, she discovered a natural talent within her.
samples.instruction: Speak with a Surprised emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'Upon', stress the word 'painting', stress the word 'discovered', and stress the word 'talent'.

samples.text: You've got to see this—it's even better than we imagined!
samples.instruction: Speak with a Happy emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'got', insert a pause after 'this', stress the word 'even', and stress the word 'better'.

samples.text: You expect me to eat this after seeing how it's prepared?
samples.instruction: Speak with a Disgusted emotion. Specifically, stress the word 'You', stress the word 'this', insert a pause after 'this', and stress the word 'prepared'.
